description: Channel or workspace dimension. Each row is a unique Frammer workspace identified by a single letter name.
semantic_text: dim_channel table stores channel or workspace information. Columns: channel_id (primary key), channel_name (label, e.g. A B C D). Each channel has a single letter name. Use this table to filter or group metrics by channel or workspace. Join to fact_user_channel and fact_channel_publishing using channel_id.

columns.name: channel_name
columns.notes: Single letter channel name e.g. A B C

notes: 18 unique channels named A through R. Use channel_name for display and channel_id for joins.

description: User dimension. Each row is a Frammer platform user who uploads or processes videos.
semantic_text: dim_user table stores user information. Columns: user_id (primary key), user_name (label, anonymized e.g. User_1 User_2). Use this table to filter or group metrics by user or uploader. Join to fact_user_channel, fact_user_summary, and fact_video using user_id.

columns.name: user_name
columns.notes: Anonymized user name e.g. User_1 User_2

notes: 45 users total. Includes real users, QA accounts, and system accounts. User names are anonymized.

description: Input content type dimension. Describes the category of source video content uploaded to Frammer before processing.
semantic_text: dim_input_type table stores input content type information. Columns: inputtype_id (primary key), input_type_name (label, e.g. interview, news bulletin, special reports, speech, debate, press conference, discussion-show, sports show, podcast, drama, in-brief). Use this table to filter or group metrics by content type or input category. Join to fact_input_type and fact_video using inputtype_id.

columns.name: input_type_name
columns.notes: Content type name e.g. interview speech news bulletin debate podcast

notes: 12 input types. fact_video.inputtype_id was derived during ETL by mapping the raw type text column to inputtype_id via dim_input_type lookup.

description: Output format type dimension. Describes the AI-generated output format produced by Frammer.
semantic_text: dim_output_type table stores output format type information. Columns: outputtype_id (primary key), output_type_name (label, e.g. Full package, Key moments, My Key moments, Chapters, Summary). Use this table to filter or group metrics by output type or video format. Join to fact_output_type using outputtype_id.

columns.name: output_type_name
columns.notes: Output format name e.g. Full package Key moments Chapters Summary

notes: 5 output types. Only joins to fact_output_type. There is no direct output type FK in fact_video.

description: Language dimension. Describes the language of the video content.
semantic_text: dim_language table stores language information. Columns: language_id (primary key), language_name (label, e.g. en, hi, mix, es, ar, mr — English, Hindi, Mixed, Spanish, Arabic, Marathi). Use this table to filter or group metrics by language or content language. Join to fact_language using language_id.

columns.name: language_name
columns.notes: Language code e.g. en hi mix es ar mr

notes: 6 languages: en=English, hi=Hindi, mix=Mixed, es=Spanish, ar=Arabic, mr=Marathi.

description: Publishing platform dimension. Describes the external social media or video platform where content was published.
semantic_text: dim_platform table stores publishing platform information. Columns: platform_id (primary key), platform_name (label, e.g. Facebook, Instagram, Linkedin, Reels, Shorts, X, Youtube, Threads, YouTube, Facebook-Reels). Use this table to filter or group publishing metrics by platform or social media destination. Join to fact_channel_publishing and fact_video using platform_id.

columns.name: platform_name
columns.notes: Platform name e.g. Facebook Instagram YouTube Reels Shorts X Threads

notes: 10 platforms. Youtube (ID=7) and YouTube (ID=9) are duplicates — use WHERE platform_name IN ('YouTube', 'Youtube') to capture both entries. fact_video.platform_id is sparsely populated and unreliable. Always use fact_channel_publishing for platform analysis.

description: Team dimension. Describes the editorial or production team responsible for the video.
semantic_text: dim_team table stores team information. Columns: team_id (primary key), team_name (label, nearly all values are Unknown). Use this table to filter or group metrics by team name. Warning: nearly all values are Unknown due to missing data quality. Join to fact_video using team_id.

columns.name: team_name
columns.notes: Team name. Nearly all values are Unknown.

notes: Only 2 rows and nearly 100% Unknown in fact_video. Always warn users that team data is a data quality issue when this dimension is queried.

description: Month time dimension for trend and time-series analysis. Covers April 2025 to February 2026.
semantic_text: dim_month table stores calendar month information for trend analysis covering April 2025 to February 2026. Columns: month_id (primary key), month_name (display label e.g. Apr 2025), month_num (1-12 for sort order), year (2025 or 2026), quarter (1-4). Use this table to filter or group metrics by month, quarter, or year. Always use ORDER BY year ASC, month_num ASC for chronological order. Join to fact_monthly using month_id.
notes: Always use ORDER BY dm.year ASC, dm.month_num ASC for correct chronological ordering. Use month_name for display labels.

description: Granular video-level fact table. Each row represents one video processed by Frammer. Contains headline source publish status type and foreign keys to user team and platform.
semantic_text: fact_video table stores individual video records. Columns: video_id (primary key), headline (video title), source (source URL or name), published (1=published, 0=not published), published_url (URL where published, often NULL), type (raw input type text), user_id (FK to dim_user), team_id (FK to dim_team, mostly Unknown), platform_id (FK to dim_platform, mostly NULL), inputtype_id (FK to dim_input_type). Use this table for video level queries, filtering by publish status, searching headlines, or counting videos per user. Do not use for aggregated counts — use pre-aggregated fact tables instead.

columns.name: source
columns.notes: Source name or URL of the video

columns.name: type
columns.notes: Raw input type text before ETL mapping

columns.name: published_url
columns.notes: URL where video was published. Often NULL or empty.

columns.name: team_id
columns.notes: FK to dim_team. Nearly all Unknown.

columns.name: platform_id
columns.notes: FK to dim_platform. Type is REAL due to ETL casting issue — treat as INTEGER. Sparsely populated, mostly NULL.

columns.name: inputtype_id
columns.notes: FK to dim_input_type. Type is REAL due to ETL casting issue — treat as INTEGER. Derived during ETL from type column.

notes: Use for video level queries only. For aggregated counts and durations use the pre-aggregated fact tables. platform_id is mostly NULL — do not use for platform analysis. team_id is mostly Unknown. Note: platform_id and inputtype_id are stored as REAL instead of INTEGER due to ETL casting — functionally equivalent for joins.

description: Aggregated fact table of usage metrics per user per channel combination. Best table for user by channel breakdown queries.
semantic_text: fact_user_channel table stores aggregated metrics per user per channel combination. Columns: uploaded_count, created_count, published_count, uploaded_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), created_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), published_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), user_id (FK to dim_user), channel_id (FK to dim_channel). Use this table for channel wise user analysis, user channel breakdown, per workspace per user metrics, uploads by user and channel, channel level performance per user.

columns.name: uploaded_count
columns.notes: Number of videos uploaded by this user in this channel

columns.name: created_count
columns.notes: Number of videos processed by this user in this channel

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Number of videos published by this user in this channel

notes: 237 rows — one per user-channel combination. Duration columns stored in seconds. Divide by 3600 for hours. Use SUM when aggregating across users or channels.

description: Aggregated fact table of total usage metrics per user across all channels. Best table for overall user level queries.
semantic_text: fact_user_summary table stores total metrics per user across all channels. Columns: uploaded_count, created_count, published_count, uploaded_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), created_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), published_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), user_id (FK to dim_user). Use this table for user level totals, top users by uploads or publishes, overall platform usage, platform-wide KPI calculations like total uploaded total published and overall publish rate.

columns.name: uploaded_count
columns.notes: Total videos uploaded by this user across all channels

columns.name: created_count
columns.notes: Total videos processed by this user across all channels

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Total videos published by this user across all channels

notes: 46 rows — one per user. Best table for platform-wide KPIs like total uploaded, total published, overall publish rate. Duration in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.

description: Monthly aggregated fact table. Each row contains total uploaded created and published counts and durations for one calendar month.
semantic_text: fact_monthly table stores monthly totals for uploaded created and published video counts and durations. Columns: total_uploaded, total_created, total_published, total_uploaded_duration (seconds), total_created_duration (seconds), total_published_duration (seconds), month_id (FK to dim_month). Use this table for monthly trends, time series analysis, monthly comparisons, quarterly rollups. Always JOIN with dim_month for month_name, year, month_num, quarter columns.

columns.name: total_uploaded
columns.notes: Total videos uploaded in this month

columns.name: total_created
columns.notes: Total videos processed in this month

columns.name: total_published
columns.notes: Total videos published in this month

columns.name: total_uploaded_duration
columns.notes: Total uploaded duration in seconds for this month. Stored in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.

columns.name: total_created_duration
columns.notes: Total created duration in seconds for this month. Stored in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.

columns.name: total_published_duration
columns.notes: Total published duration in seconds for this month. Stored in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.

notes: 12 rows — one per month from Apr 2025 to Feb 2026. Always JOIN with dim_month and ORDER BY dm.year ASC, dm.month_num ASC for correct chronological order.

description: Channel by platform publishing fact table. Each row contains published count and duration for one channel and platform combination.
semantic_text: fact_channel_publishing table stores published video counts and durations per channel per platform. Columns: published_count, published_duration (seconds), channel_id (FK to dim_channel), platform_id (FK to dim_platform). Use this table for platform analysis, channel platform breakdown, which platform a channel publishes to, published hours by platform, published count by social media. This is the most reliable table for platform analysis — do not use fact_video.platform_id.

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Number of videos published for this channel-platform combination

columns.name: published_duration
columns.notes: Total published duration in seconds for this channel-platform combination

notes: 145 rows — one per channel-platform pair. This is the most reliable table for platform analysis. Do not use fact_video.platform_id for platform queries as it is sparsely populated.

description: Input type aggregated fact table. Each row contains uploaded created and published counts and durations for one input content type.
semantic_text: fact_input_type table stores aggregated metrics per input content type. Columns: uploaded_count, created_count, published_count, uploaded_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), created_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), published_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), inputtype_id (FK to dim_input_type). Use this table for input type breakdown, content category analysis, publish funnel by content type such as interview speech news bulletin debate podcast drama.

columns.name: uploaded_count
columns.notes: Total videos uploaded for this input type

columns.name: created_count
columns.notes: Total videos processed for this input type

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Total videos published for this input type

notes: 12 rows — one per input type. Duration in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours. Best table for input type level aggregated queries.

description: Language aggregated fact table. Each row contains uploaded created and published counts and durations for one language.
semantic_text: fact_language table stores aggregated metrics per language. Columns: uploaded_count, created_count, published_count, uploaded_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), created_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), published_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), language_id (FK to dim_language). Use this table for language breakdown, language wise upload or publish counts, and language publish rate analysis for languages like English Hindi Spanish Arabic Marathi mixed.

columns.name: uploaded_count
columns.notes: Total videos uploaded for this language

columns.name: created_count
columns.notes: Total videos processed for this language

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Total videos published for this language

notes: 6 rows — one per language. Duration in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.

description: Output type aggregated fact table. Each row contains uploaded created and published counts and durations for one output format type.
semantic_text: fact_output_type table stores aggregated metrics per output format type. Columns: uploaded_count, created_count, published_count, uploaded_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), created_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), published_duration_hh_mm_ss (seconds), outputtype_id (FK to dim_output_type). Use this table for output type breakdown, format distribution, and publish rate by output format such as Full package Key moments My Key moments Chapters Summary.

columns.name: uploaded_count
columns.notes: Total videos uploaded for this output type

columns.name: created_count
columns.notes: Total videos processed for this output type

columns.name: published_count
columns.notes: Total videos published for this output type

notes: 5 rows — one per output type. Duration in seconds — divide by 3600 for hours.
